description: A vertical designed graphic from the U.S. Department of Labor presenting a roundup of four local-news headlines on a dark navy background (with a faint workshop image faintly visible behind). At the top, a bold mixed-serif title reads 'America is Investing in OUR FUTURE SKILLED WORKFORCE!' with 'America is Investing in' in white italic, 'OUR FUTURE SKILLED' in white caps, and 'WORKFORCE!' in very large white caps. Below are four white 'torn-paper' style cards arranged in a 2x2 grid, each with a small news-outlet logo header and an italic headline: (top-left) 'MONTANA.GOV OFFICIAL STATE WEBSITE' - 'Montana, RDO Launch New Skilled Trade Apprenticeship Program'; (top-right) 'WCIA.com' - 'Parkland College Launches New Healthcare Pre-Apprenticeship Program'; (bottom-left) '2 WMAR abc BALTIMORE' - 'Baltimore City Apprenticeship Program Holds 4th Graduation'; (bottom-right) '7 KLTV abc' - 'Congressman Moran Plans WAGES Act to Expand Apprenticeship Programs in East Texas.' A small embossed Department of Labor seal sits centered at the bottom. The style aggregates real local-media headlines to create an impression of broad, nationwide positive coverage. The messaging promotes the Trump administration's apprenticeship/skilled-workforce agenda via a 'look at all this good press' bandwagon technique. Target audience is the general public and workforce stakeholders.
notable_text: America is Investing in OUR FUTURE SKILLED WORKFORCE! / Montana, RDO Launch New Skilled Trade Apprenticeship Program / Parkland College Launches New Healthcare Pre-Apprenticeship Program / Baltimore City Apprenticeship Program Holds 4th Graduation / Congressman Moran Plans WAGES Act to Expand Apprenticeship Programs in East Texas
